subtasks.description: Query SQL warehouse to retrieve Datadog license allocations by type (monitoring, APM, log management, etc.), seat counts, contract terms, and entitlements

Query SQL warehouse to retrieve Datadog actual usage metrics including consumption rates, active monitors, API calls, log ingestion volume, APM traces, and other resource utilization data over recent periods

Reconcile license allocations with actual usage metrics, calculate utilization rates, identify growth patterns (month-over-month, quarter-over-quarter), detect over/under-provisioning, and classify usage posture (underutilized, at-capacity, oversubscribed). Generate trend analysis and recommendations.

plan.cost_rationale: Three subtasks: (1) retrieve_license_allocation and (2) retrieve_usage_metrics are separate SQL queries on different entities with different schemas and aggregation logic, so they cannot be combined into one sql_gen call. (3) cross_recon performs the core analytical work—reconciling the two datasets, calculating utilization gaps, detecting growth patterns, and generating posture classification and recommendations. Tier assignments: sql_gen tasks are mid (multi-table joins across contract/entitlement and usage tables with time-based aggregations); cross_recon is frontier because it requires multi-signal reconciliation, time-series trend detection, anomaly identification (over/under-provisioning), and recommendations—high analytical complexity deserving more capable reasoning.

output.sql: SELECT cf.product, cf.event_type, SUM(cf.daily_active_seats) AS total_active_seats, COUNT(DISTINCT cf.license_id) AS license_count, SUM(cf.amount_usd) AS total_amount_usd, AVG(cf.daily_active_seats) AS avg_daily_active_seats, MIN(cf.event_date) AS first_event_date, MAX(cf.event_date) AS last_event_date, cf.fiscal_year, cf.fiscal_quarter, COUNT(DISTINCT cf.user_id) AS unique_users, SUM(cf.amount_usd) / NULLIF(SUM(cf.daily_active_seats), 0) AS cost_per_seat FROM consumption_facts cf WHERE cf.publisher ILIKE '%datadog%' GROUP BY cf.product, cf.event_type, cf.fiscal_year, cf.fiscal_quarter ORDER BY cf.fiscal_year ASC, cf.fiscal_quarter ASC, cf.product ASC, total_amount_usd DESC

SELECT publisher, product, fiscal_year, fiscal_quarter, SUM(amount_usd) AS total_amount_usd, SUM(daily_active_seats) AS total_daily_active_seats, COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) AS distinct_users, COUNT(event_id) AS total_events, AVG(amount_usd) AS avg_amount_usd, AVG(daily_active_seats) AS avg_daily_active_seats, MIN(event_date) AS earliest_event, MAX(event_date) AS latest_event, SUM(CASE WHEN event_type = 'consumption' THEN amount_usd ELSE 0 END) AS consumption_amount, SUM(CASE WHEN event_type = 'overage' THEN amount_usd ELSE 0 END) AS overage_amount, COUNT(DISTINCT license_id) AS distinct_licenses FROM consumption_facts WHERE LOWER(publisher) LIKE '%datadog%' AND event_date >= '2023-01-01' GROUP BY publisher, product, fiscal_year, fiscal_quarter ORDER BY fiscal_year DESC, fiscal_quarter DESC, total_amount_usd DESC

output.note: Upstream outputs contain only SQL schema definitions without actual result rows; no reconcilable entity data was provided. Returning placeholder structure based on schema.
